entries.reasoning: Illustrative example of the strategy with a clear favourite. Pred 2 (regulation) is the most likely 90' result — a decisive Spain win — while Pred 3 only pays if the game is level after 90, so it targets the modal extra-time draw. The two deliberately differ.

Worked example on a real, recognisable result: the 2022 final. An evenly-matched final carries huge draw mass, so the EV-optimal regulation and extra-time picks can coincide on the modal draw — and the match really did finish 2-2, then 3-3, before Argentina won on penalties.

Canada are clear favorites with superior attacking quality through Jonathan David and Cyle Larin, and the market makes them strong favorites (away 0.569), though they're missing Alphonso Davies and Ismael Kone. South Africa are a disciplined, defensive counter-attacking side that has overperformed, so low-scoring outcomes and draws carry meaningful mass in this nervy first-ever knockout tie for both nations. Several analysts flag a realistic path to extra time and even a South Africa penalty shootout win, so I keep draw probability high and tilt the advance edge to Canada without making it ove

Brazil are clear favorites with Vinicius in superb form and Neymar available, while Japan are missing playmaker Kubo but remain elite defensively, having conceded just three goals in 540 minutes. Japan are organized and dangerous on the counter, so a cagey, low-scoring tie with realistic draw mass is likely. Brazil's superior firepower should edge it, but multiple previews tip a potential extra-time marathon given Japan's resilience.

Germany are heavy favorites but enter off a loss to Ecuador and have shown attacking issues, while Paraguay are deeply defensive with the second-lowest group-stage xG and openly target extra time. The Schlotterbeck loss and Brown doubt slightly weaken Germany's defense, but Paraguay's suspension of Diego Gomez and doubt over Alderete hurt them more. Expect a low-scoring, grind-it-out match with realistic draw mass and elevated chance of extra time, with Germany most likely winning by a tight margin like 1-0 or 2-1.

Both teams are fully fit and attack-minded — Netherlands and Morocco rank top-14 in xG/90, and neither has kept a clean sheet at the tournament, so both-teams-to-score and over 2.5 goals are strongly favored. The Netherlands are a slight de-vigged favorite (~0.41) with superior squad depth, but Morocco's quality and proven knockout pedigree (penalty win vs Spain in 2022) keep them dangerous and elevate draw/extra-time probability. Regulation draws carry meaningful mass given the open, high-scoring nature, and Morocco's shootout reputation narrows the advance gap relative to the 90-minute line.

Norway are slight favorites with Haaland and Odegaard rested and fresh for the knockouts after sitting out the France loss, giving them match-winning quality despite a shaky defense. Ivory Coast are well-organized, hard to break down and dangerous in transition, but their finishing has been a recurring weakness, missing injured right-back Singo though Ndicka returns fit. Bookmaker odds and previews frame this as a genuine coin flip leaning narrowly to Norway, so regulation draws carry strong mass and extra time is a realistic outcome.

France are overwhelming favorites with the tournament's deepest attack (Mbappé, Dembélé, Olise) and a perfect group stage, while Sweden scraped in as a third-placed team and are missing centre-back Isak Hien, leaving their defense vulnerable. However, Sweden's strike pair of Isak and Gyökeres can punish France on the counter, so a France win with a Sweden goal is the most likely outcome. Regulation draw mass is kept moderate given the talent gap, but knockout caution and Sweden's threat keep extra-time probability meaningful at roughly the de-vigged draw price.

Mexico won Group A with three straight clean sheets and home advantage at a packed Estadio Azteca, where they are virtually unbeaten, supporting their de-vigged edge. Ecuador have an elite defense (Pacho, Hincapie, Caicedo) but have struggled to finish all tournament, so this projects as a tight, low-scoring knockout with strong draw mass and a real chance of extra time. Both sides have settled XIs with no fresh injury concerns, and given two stout defenses, scoreless and one-goal margins dominate the distribution. Mexico is favored to edge it, but the small individual-quality gap keeps Ecuado

England are heavy favourites (market ~72% for the win) as Group L winners with Harry Kane in record-breaking form, though their attack has been inconsistent and they face a right-back crisis with Reece James (hamstring) and Jarell Quansah (ankle) both out, forcing Djed Spence to start. DR Congo are a resilient, physical diaspora-built side (Wissa, Wan-Bissaka, Mbemba) making their first-ever knockout appearance with no reported injuries, and Desabre may deploy a defensive 5-3-2, raising modest chances of a low-scoring game or upset. Low-scoring England wins (1-0, 2-0, 2-1) carry most mass, wit

This is widely regarded as the coin-flip of the round: Belgium have the bigger names but a misfiring attack that only clicked against New Zealand, while Senegal are fast in transition yet defensively fragile and missing keeper Edouard Mendy. Market odds (0.437/0.292/0.271) and Belgium's slight quality/depth edge (Doku confirmed to start) tilt advancement modestly their way, but Senegal's goal threat keeps this open. Goals look likely on both ends, so I've given draws realistic mass and kept scorelines open, with meaningful probability of extra time.

USA are heavy home favorites at Levi's Stadium with Pulisic returning from a calf injury and Richards anchoring defense, backed by de-vigged odds (0.69) and Opta's ~67.5% win projection. Bosnia progressed as a best third-placed team but were defensively shaky, never keeping a clean sheet in World Cup history, though physically tall and dangerous from set-pieces with Dzeko and Demirovic. USA's attack (Balogun, Pulisic) has scored freely but their defense conceded three to Turkey, keeping draw/upset mass realistic. Knockout stakes give meaningful extra-time probability (~20%), with low-scoring d

Spain are heavy favorites, unbeaten in 34 matches and yet to concede at this World Cup, while Austria's high press has been thrilling but leaky (no clean sheet in 12 straight WC games). Spain's attack is depleted with Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino likely out, but Lamine Yamal is fit and the midfield of Rodri/Pedri should control the game. Draw mass is realistic given Austria's attacking threat and Spain's sluggish start, so extra time carries meaningful probability. If it goes to ET, Spain's superior quality and depth make them strong favorites to win or advance on penalties.

Both teams are at full strength with no injuries or suspensions, and Portugal recall first-choice players including Ronaldo, Bruno Fernandes and Joao Neves after group-stage rotation. Portugal are clear favourites per de-vigged market (55.7%) with superior depth, but Croatia's midfield of Modric and Kovacic plus their renowned knockout resilience keeps this competitive and raises draw/ET risk. Portugal's advance probability exceeds their 90' win probability because they'd be favoured in extra time and penalties. Low-scoring, tight scorelines carry the most mass given both defenses and Croatia'

Switzerland topped Group B unbeaten with 13 goals and looks the sharper, more disciplined side, while Algeria has conceded seven goals in three group games showing clear defensive fragility. Manzambi's form and Xhaka's control favor the Swiss, though Mahrez and Algeria's attack should threaten. Market and form point to a Swiss edge but knockout tension keeps draws (0-0, 1-1) meaningful, giving a real chance of extra time. Both squads appear largely fit, with Amoura a doubt for Algeria.

Both are defensively organized, low-scoring sides—Australia scored just twice in the group stage and Egypt is watertight at the back, pointing to a tight, cagey match with heavy draw mass. Egypt is the slight favorite via superior individual quality (Salah is cleared to play but his minutes will be managed, plus Marmoush), though they're missing Fattouh and suspended Lasheen with Abdelmonem doubtful. Low expected goals and knockout stakes give a meaningful chance of extra time. The advance edge tilts to Egypt but Australia's structure keeps them live.

Argentina are overwhelming favorites with Messi in devastating form (6 goals) and Romero fit, while Cape Verde's elite defense (Vozinha, three group draws including clean sheets vs Spain and Saudi Arabia) points to a low-scoring game. A single-goal Argentina win is most likely, but Cape Verde's proven ability to frustrate top sides gives realistic draw mass and a modest chance of extra time. If it reaches extra time, Argentina's superior depth and quality make them clear favorites to eventually break through or advance on penalties.

Colombia are clear favorites (market ~66%), boasting a far superior attack and defense than a Ghana side that has scored just twice all tournament and defends in a deep low block, with top threat Semenyo an ankle doubt. Both teams are strongly under-friendly, so a low-scoring Colombia win (1-0 most likely) dominates, but Ghana's discipline gives realistic mass to draws and a meaningful chance of extra time. If it reaches ET, Colombia's superior quality should eventually break through, though a shootout remains plausible.

Morocco are clear favorites with an intact, elite defense (allowing under 1.0 xGA per game) and strong knockout pedigree, matching the market's ~52% regulation win and Opta's 51.8% simulation edge. Canada get a home crowd boost with Alphonso Davies likely making his first start plus in-form Jonathan David, keeping them live as underdogs. Morocco's stifling defense and both teams' late-drama tendencies point to a low-scoring game with heavy draw mass, so a 0-0 or 1-1 into extra time is very plausible. Given Morocco's shootout mastery (Bounou), if it reaches penalties they are strong favorites t

France are heavy favorites (de-vigged ~79% and Opta ~78.8% regulation win) with a rampant attack that has scored two-plus goals in 16 of their last 17 matches, while Paraguay sit deep and rely on shootout hero Orlando Gill and counters. Paraguay's grinding, low-scoring style (multiple 0-0/1-1 draws) skews probable outcomes toward narrow France margins, though upsets are possible if they nick an early goal as against Germany. Extra time probability is modest (~13-15%), reflecting France's likelihood of winning in 90; conditional ET scores favor low totals given Paraguay's defensive setup but wi

Brazil are clear favorites given superior depth and momentum, but they're weakened by the absence of Raphinha and Paquetá, with Endrick/Rayan filling in. Norway are fully fit and dangerous through in-form Haaland (five goals) and Ødegaard, and have scored and conceded in every match this tournament, so both teams finding the net is likely. The de-vigged market (home 0.537) plus knockout caution supports meaningful draw mass and a real chance of extra time, where Brazil's individual quality still tips advancement in their favor.

Mexico is unbeaten and yet to concede at the tournament, backed by a euphoric Azteca crowd and a 2,200m altitude edge that saps visiting teams, though England's individual quality (Kane, Bellingham, Saka) makes them slight market favorites at 0.363. England carry a genuine right-back crisis with James and Quansah injured, forcing makeshift solutions against Mexico's relentless press. Both sides defend well and England have looked laboured, so I weight low-scoring lines and give the 0-0/1-1 draws realistic mass, pushing a meaningful extra-time probability. In a knockout that goes to the wire I

Spain enter as clear favorites (de-vigged ~49% away, Opta ~48.6% inside 90'), unbeaten in 34 with four clean sheets and controlling midfield, while Portugal have looked defensively vulnerable and lack attacking fluency. Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino are doubtful for Spain but an otherwise strong XI is expected; Portugal are fully fit. Low-scoring outcomes carry heavy mass given Spain's stinginess and the historically tight head-to-head, so 0-0, 1-1 and 1-0 results are prominent. Portugal's shootout pedigree (beat Spain on penalties in the 2025 Nations League final) lifts their advance chance a

Balogun's suspension was overturned, restoring USA's leading scorer, and with home advantage at Lumen Field the Americans are now slight favorites over an aging Belgium side whose stars (Lukaku, De Bruyne, Doku) have struggled for fitness and form. Belgium retains individual quality and big-game pedigree, so this is close to a coin-flip. Both teams' cohesion and defensive solidity give meaningful draw mass, and knockout tension makes extra time a real possibility (~31%), where USA's crowd and bench tilt things marginally their way.

Argentina enter in dominant form with four straight wins and a healthy squad including a fit Messi and Romero, and the de-vigged market makes them heavy favorites (0.678). Egypt reached this round via a penalty shootout over Australia with Salah managing a hamstring issue, so they are more likely to sit deep and defend than win outright. Regulation mass tilts strongly to narrow-to-comfortable Argentina wins, with realistic draw mass reflecting knockout caution. If it reaches extra time, Argentina's superior depth favors them edging a low-scoring 120-minute result.

Both sides are defensively solid, low-scoring and reached the last 16 unbeaten, so draws carry real mass in what previews call the tightest tie of the round. Colombia are marginal favorites (market ~43% regulation win, model ~52% to advance) with Luis Diaz the likely difference-maker, though they lost striker Jhon Cordoba to injury and James Rodriguez was managing illness. Switzerland lose creative No.10 Manzambi and possibly Aebischer/Jaquez, blunting their attack, but keep an efficient spine around Xhaka and Embolo. Given the tactical, low-scoring nature and knockout format, extra time is a

France are clear favorites and the only team to win all five matches so far, with Mbappe in Golden Boot form, and the market prices them around 60% in 90 minutes plus extra-time/penalty edge pushing advancement near 71%. Morocco lose top scorer Saibari to a hamstring injury, blunting their transition threat, though they bring an elite defense and a 34-match unbeaten run that keeps low-scoring and draw outcomes live. Regulation draws (0-0, 1-1) carry meaningful mass given Morocco's defensive record and knockout caution, so extra time is a realistic ~26%. If it goes to extra time, tight one- and

Spain enter as heavy favorites, unbeaten and yet to concede all tournament with a record clean-sheet run behind Unai Simon, and their possession-based control with Rodri, Pedri and in-form Oyarzabal suits low-scoring, tight wins. Belgium lost midfielder Amadou Onana to an ACL injury, weakening their defensive shielding, though De Bruyne and Courtois give them genuine upset potential on transitions. Spain's stingy defense makes low-scoring outcomes (1-0, 0-0, clean-sheet wins) most likely, while knockout tension supports meaningful draw mass and a moderate chance of extra time. Advance probabil

Market makes England clear favorites (de-vigged ~49% win vs Norway 25%), reflecting deeper squad quality despite Norway's Haaland-fueled run and both Guéhi and Rice passing fitness tests to start. Norway have both scored and conceded in all five games and haven't kept a clean sheet, while Haaland has scored in every match, so goals are likely at both ends and a low-scoring shutout is unlikely. I weighted draws realistically given knockout tension and two elite strikers, with 1-1 and narrow England wins most probable. Argentina are clear favourites (market 0.556) and progress with high probability including shootout edge behind Emi Martinez, but recent knockout games show real defensive frailty against direct, pacey opponents which Switzerland can exploit, especially if Manzambi returns. Romero is a knee doubt and Medina is managing fatigue, adding uncertainty to Argentina's backline. Switzerland are defensively disciplined (0-0 vs Colombia), so low-scoring outcomes and draws carry meaningful mass, keeping extra-time probability around the market draw figure of 0.267.
